mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is suitable for applications requiring fast similarity search and filtering capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient resources to handle large datasets and the complexity of managing persistent storage.

steps.title: Add Qdrant Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Qdrant Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Qdrant using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add qdrant https://qdrant.github.io/qdrant-helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Qdrant Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Qdrant with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Qdrant in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --create-namespace --version 1.13.0

```

> Replace '1.13.0' with the desired version if needed.

steps.title: Create a Qdrant Configuration File
steps.description:
This step creates a configuration file for Qdrant, which defines storage paths and logging settings.

```bash

kubectl create configmap qdrant-config --from-file=config.yaml --namespace qdrant

```

> Ensure you have a config.yaml file ready with your desired configuration.

steps.title: Verify Qdrant Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Qdrant pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Look for the status 'Running' for all Qdrant pods.

steps.title: Expose Qdrant Service
steps.description:
This step exposes the Qdrant service to allow external access.

```bash

kubectl expose deployment qdrant --type=LoadBalancer --name=qdrant-service --port=6333 --target-port=6333 --namespace=qdrant

```

> This creates a LoadBalancer service for external access.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Qdrant deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall qdrant --namespace qdrant

kubectl delete namespace qdrant

```

> This command removes the Qdrant release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n qdrant -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --version 1.13.0

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Drains the pods to prepare for upgrade.
> Replace '1.13.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n qdrant -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be exposed correctly or the pods are not running.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the pods with 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' and ensure the service is exposed correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The persistent volume may not have enough storage allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the storage size in your persistent volume claim or adjust the storage configuration in config.yaml.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Qdrant pod is crashing repeatedly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to misconfiguration in the config.yaml file.

**Fix:**
Review the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n qdrant' and correct any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused on port 6333'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be running or the port is not exposed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Qdrant service is running and properly exposed using 'kubectl get svc -n qdrant'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old snapshot storage configuration in favor of a more flexible approach.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.12.x, update your config.yaml to reflect the new storage paths.

resolution.summary: You should see the Qdrant service running and accessible via the LoadBalancer IP with the command 'kubectl get svc -n qdrant'. The output should show the EXTERNAL-IP of the service.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
